title: Humulopsis scandens
taxonomy: Humulopsis scandens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a member of the genus Humulopsis, which is a group of plants native to the tropical regions of the world. Humulopsis scandens is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is a type of climber or twiner, which uses its stems to support itself as it grows upwards. Further classification of Humulopsis scandens can be broken down into its kingdom, phylum, class, and order, but its specific classification within those categories is less commonly discussed.
uses: Humulopsis scandens has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, for its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various infections.
